zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan le Bigod於大約1248年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾可廷加姆時，她的父親Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England37歲，母親Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham34歲。她於大約1270年與Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1320年在英國林可爾史爾諾斯 吉享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾諾斯 吉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan le Bigod于大约1248年出生在英国英格兰越可爾可廷加姆时，她的父亲Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England37岁，母亲Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham34岁。她于大约1270年与Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1320年在英国林可爾史爾諾斯 吉享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾諾斯 吉。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan le Bigod was born about 1248, in Cottingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England, was 37 and her mother, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, was 34. She married Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme about 1270. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1320, in North Kyme, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in North Kyme, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan le Bigod est née environ 1248, à Cottingham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme environ 1270. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1320, à North Kyme, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à North Kyme, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan le Bigod um 1248 in Cottingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England 37 und ihre Mutter Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham 34. Sie hat um 1270 Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1320 im Alter von 74 Jahren in North Kyme, Lincolnshire, England und wurde in North Kyme, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme circa 1270. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Joan le Bigodが約1248年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアコッティンガムで生まれた時，父Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of Englandは37歳，母Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottinghamは34歳であった。約1270年にPhilip de Kyme 1st Baron Kymeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1320年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアノース クイメで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアノース クイメにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan le Bigod(이)가 대략 1248년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 컷잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1270년 Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1320년 영국 링코실르 노르스 키에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 노르스 키에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme aproximadamente 1270. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan le Bigod родилась около 1248 г. в местности коттингхам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh Bigod Chief Justice of England было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip de Kyme 1st Baron Kyme около 1270 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1320 г. в местности норт ким, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности норт ким, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
